Research Areas

Core Research Directions

Our center integrates interdisciplinary sciences, focusing on extreme disasters under climate change. From technical monitoring and impact assessment to building societal resilience, we are committed to developing sustainable solutions to address global challenges of drought, heat stress, and water resources.

research.exploreWork

PublicationsClick to explore our findings

Drought Monitoring and Early Warning Technology

Developing high-resolution, high-precision drought monitoring and early warning systems to assess the cascading impacts of drought on water resources, agriculture, ecosystems, and the socio-economy.

Keywords
Satellite Remote SensingDrought IndicesAI & Big DataDecision Support SystemsImpact AssessmentGPS Meteorology

Urban Heat Island Effect and Climate Adaptation

Focusing on the threat of extreme high temperatures to public health, exploring the causes and monitoring of the urban heat island effect, and developing nature-based urban cooling and health protection strategies.

Keywords
Urban Heat IslandUrban Climate ModelingSmart CitiesGreen BuildingsPublic HealthLand Use

Climate Policy and Water Resource Management

Analyzing the impact of climate change on the water cycle, exploring innovative strategies for water supply and demand management, and collaborative governance frameworks for multiple stakeholders.

Keywords
Water GovernanceSocietal ResilienceSmart Water GridsWater ReuseCross-boundary ManagementDecision Science

Health Impacts of Climate Change

Assessing the effects of extreme weather on cardiovascular diseases, infectious diseases, food security, and mental health, and promoting the construction of a climate-resilient healthcare and public health system.

Keywords
Public HealthHealth Risk AssessmentEpidemicsHealth Information ManagementNanotoxicologyInfection & Immunity

Economic Impact Assessment of Extreme Climate

Quantifying the impact of extreme weather on industrial supply chains, energy markets, and finance, and developing innovative risk-sharing mechanisms and financial response solutions.

Keywords
Economic Risk AssessmentSupply Chain ManagementDisaster Risk ModelingESG FrameworkClimate FinanceConsumer Behavior

Interdisciplinary Technology Integration and Application

Integrating cutting-edge technologies such as space optics, planetary remote sensing, neurolinguistics, and computational linguistics to explore new models for climate data analysis and human-computer interaction.

Keywords
Space OpticsPlanetary Remote SensingNeurolinguisticsHuman-Computer InteractionInformation InnovationMorphology
